message.content:
**For most people with healthy, normal skin, get a professional facial every 4-6 weeks.**[1][2][5][6] This frequency aligns with the skin's natural renewal cycle, allowing time for cell turnover while preventing buildup of dead skin, dullness, or breakouts.[1][4]

### Variations by Skin Type
Frequency should be personalized based on skin type, concerns, age, and esthetician advice—most sources emphasize consulting a professional.[2][5]
- **Oily or acne-prone skin**: Every 2-4 weeks initially to control sebum, clogs, and breakouts, then monthly for maintenance.[1][2][4]
- **Dry, sensitive, or dehydrated skin**: Every 5-8 weeks to avoid irritation and restore moisture.[1][2]
- **Combination or normal skin**: Every 4-6 weeks (or monthly).[1][2][4][6]
- **Mature skin (40+)**: Every 3-4 weeks to boost collagen and counter slower cell turnover.[1][2]

### Variations by Concern or Age
| Concern/Age Group | Recommended Frequency | Notes |
|-------------------|-----------------------|-------|
| Acne/Congestion | Every 2-3 weeks initially, then monthly[2][4] | Focus on extractions. |
| Hyperpigmentation | Monthly for mild peels[2] | Space stronger treatments. |
| Fine lines/Wrinkles | Every 3-4 weeks[2] | Hydration and firming. |
| Rosacea/Redness | Every 6-8 weeks[2] | Gentle, calming treatments. |
| Teens (13-19) | Every 4-6 weeks[2] | Targets acne and pores. |
| 20s-30s | Every 4-6 weeks[2] | Hydration and prevention. |

One source suggests every 3 months to avoid irritation from overly frequent sessions,[3] but this is less common and contradicted by most experts favoring 4-6 weeks.[1][2][5] If budget limits access, aim for at least quarterly (4 times/year).[4] Regular facials improve circulation, relaxation, and glow, but overdoing them risks redness or sensitivity.[2][3][4]
